assertions.title: Settings page displays OAuth Connections section

steps.expected: Page loads with OAuth Connections section visible
steps.observed: Page loaded successfully. Found section with class 'section-title' containing 'OAuth Connections' heading, description text, and two backend entries (codex and copilot).

steps.action: Verify at least one backend entry is listed
steps.expected: At least one backend entry visible in OAuth section
steps.observed: Two backend entries found: 'codex' (type: codex) and 'copilot' (type: copilot), each in their own .oauth-backend-card div.

assertions.title: Each OAuth backend shows connected status

steps.action: Inspect OAuth backend cards for status indicators
steps.expected: Each backend row shows a status label (Connected or Not connected)
steps.observed: Both backend cards contain status elements: codex shows 'Status: Not connected' with class 'oauth-text-missing', copilot shows 'Status: Not connected' with class 'oauth-text-missing'. Each card has a status dot element (.oauth-status-dot) and a .oauth-detail-value span.

assertions.title: Token expiry time displayed for authenticated backends

steps.action: Check if any OAuth backends are in connected state
steps.expected: At least one backend is connected to check expiry display
steps.observed: Both backends (codex and copilot) show 'Not connected' status. No backends are in authenticated state. The template at oauth_status.html contains conditional logic to display 'Expires:' label with .TokenExpiry value when .Authenticated is true, but this section is not rendered because no backends are connected.

assertions.issues: Blocked: No OAuth backends are currently connected. The codex backend requires a real OpenAI OAuth flow, and the copilot backend requires an active GitHub Copilot subscription with valid token exchange. The template code exists to display expiry time when a backend is authenticated (via .TokenExpiry field), but cannot be verified without a connected backend.

assertions.title: Last refresh time displayed for authenticated backends

steps.action: Check if any OAuth backends are in connected state
steps.expected: At least one backend is connected to check last refresh display
steps.observed: Both backends show 'Not connected'. The template at oauth_status.html contains conditional logic to display 'Last refreshed:' with .LastRefresh value when .Authenticated is true, but this section is not rendered because no backends are connected.

assertions.issues: Blocked: No OAuth backends are currently connected. Same reason as VAL-UI-003. Template code exists to display last refresh time when a backend is authenticated.

assertions.title: Copilot backend shows discovered token source

steps.action: Check copilot backend card for token source label
steps.expected: Copilot backend row shows token source (e.g., 'Source: gh auth token')
steps.observed: Copilot backend shows 'Not connected' status. The template code shows that the 'Source:' label with .TokenSource value is only displayed when .Authenticated is true (inside the {{if .Authenticated}} block). Since the copilot backend is not connected, the token source information is not rendered.

assertions.issues: Blocked: Copilot backend is not connected. The template structure correctly includes a 'Source:' meta item that displays .TokenSource when the backend is authenticated, but this requires a successful Copilot token exchange which depends on an active GitHub Copilot subscription.

assertions.title: Navigate to /ui/settings and see the OAuth section

steps.expected: Page loads with OAuth Connections section immediately visible
steps.observed: Navigating to /ui/settings renders the full settings page with the OAuth Connections section visible in the default view. No tabs, modals, or additional clicks required. The section appears between 'Statistics' and 'Anonymous API Keys' sections.

assertions.title: OAuth status accessible without separate UI authentication

steps.action: Open /ui/settings directly in browser without any prior login
steps.expected: Page loads with full OAuth details, no login wall
steps.observed: The page loaded immediately showing all OAuth backend details (codex and copilot status, Connect button, Check Status button, full configuration data including the raw config in the modal). No login prompt or authentication step was encountered. The web UI is unauthenticated.

steps.action: Navigate to /ui/settings and check OAuth Connections section
steps.expected: Both Copilot and Codex backends listed with independent statuses and controls
steps.observed: The OAuth Connections section lists two distinct backend entries: (1) 'codex' with type badge 'codex', status 'Not connected', and a 'Connect' link; (2) 'copilot' with type badge 'copilot', status 'Not connected', and a 'Check Status' button. Both entries have independent status dots, names, type badges, and action buttons. They are in separate .oauth-backend-card divs.

blockers.description: VAL-UI-003, VAL-UI-004, VAL-UI-005 are blocked because no OAuth backends are currently in connected state. The copilot backend requires an active GitHub Copilot subscription for token exchange to succeed. The codex backend requires completing a real OpenAI OAuth PKCE flow. Both show 'Not connected' which prevents testing of authenticated-state UI elements (expiry time, last refresh, token source).
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Checked that gh CLI is authenticated (confirmed). The copilot backend still shows 'Not connected' — likely because the GitHub token lacks Copilot-specific scopes or the Copilot token exchange endpoint requires an active Copilot subscription.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 5 passed, 0 failed, 3 blocked (VAL-UI-003, VAL-UI-004, VAL-UI-005 — blocked due to no connected OAuth backends). The OAuth Connections section is properly rendered on /ui/settings with both codex and copilot backends visible, each showing clear status indicators and appropriate action buttons. The page is accessible without authentication and loads immediately without requiring additional navigation. HTMX auto-refresh is configured with 30-second polling.
